title: , Bishop of Amathus and wonderworker
name: Tychon
troparia: (Tone 4) You first crucified yourself to the world by becoming poor among the living.* Then like a true shepherd, you put your life at the disposal of your flock.* Enriching your sheep with miracles, you became a bright light for the Church of Amathus.* By shedding light on it, you saved it from the fog of deceit, O father Tychon.* By our prayers enlighten us today as we praise you.
kontakia: (Tone 3) You persevered in God-pleasing fasting, O holy one,* and acquired from on high the power of the Comforter* to overthrow the lies of idolatry, to case away demons, to heal infirmities.* Therefore we venerate you as a friend of God,** O blessed Tychon.
